mnemonics.title: Function Prototype Syntax
mnemonics.content: REMEMBER: TYPE FIRST, THEN NAME, PARENTHESES WITH PARAMETERS, END WITH SEMICOLON. Think: TELL THE COMPILER WHAT, THEN WHO, THEN HOW, THEN DONE.

mnemonics.title: Prototype vs Definition
mnemonics.content: PROTOTYPE DECLARES (says what), DEFINITION DEFINES (does the work). Declaration gives signature, Definition gives body.

mnemonics.title: Void Parameters
mnemonics.content: EMPTY PARENS () = UNSPECIFIED, VOID PARENS (void) = NO ARGUMENTS. Use void to be explicit.

acronyms.expansion: Pre-declaration Of Return Type, Parameter Order, Name, Type, User-defined Elements (informal mnemonic)
acronyms.usage: Remembering what information a prototype provides to the compiler
The C standard that standardized function prototypes in 1989

visualTips.visualization: Think of a function prototype as a NAME TAG: return_type (the title) function_name (the name on the tag) parameters (the qualifications listed). Just as a name tag tells people about you before you speak, a prototype tells the compiler about the function before it executes.

Draw a timeline: headers at top, then prototypes, then main, then functions. Like a PLAYBILL: first the actors are announced (prototypes), then the main show begins (main), then the actual performances (definitions).

analogies.concept: Function Prototype as a Recipe Card
analogies.analogy: A function prototype is like a recipe card in a cookbook that lists ingredients (parameters) and what the dish will be (return type) before the actual cooking instructions (function body) appear. This lets you know what to expect and prepare before cooking starts.

A function prototype is like a warning sign on the road ahead - it tells you what's coming (function name), what type of vehicle to expect (return type), and what conditions apply (parameters), allowing you to prepare accordingly.

Think of a function declaration (prototype) as a person introducing themselves at a party saying 'Hi, I'm John, I play the guitar' - they're announcing what they can do. The actual performance later is like the function definition - that's when they actually play the guitar.
